content.full_text:
[handwritten at top of page]
CITY / UNINCORPORATED LIMITED TO SOURCES ITEMS
within the jurisdiction.

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                           Date : March 6, 1978

[handwritten]
A1-78-360

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: Coastal Commission Code


The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts
from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commission need not be disclosed. The justification for this
decision was that disclosure under this Code already was very
broad. The Committee thought that this type of jurisdictional
limitation would be appropriate for Codes in which broad disclosure already has been imposed upon the highest officials and
those lower officials who have much contact with persons outside
the jurisdiction.


TP:mfa

sections.question_synthetic: Whether gifts from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commission need to be disclosed under the Coastal Commission Code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts from sources outside the Coastal Commission's jurisdiction need not be disclosed, based on the rationale that disclosure requirements under the Code are already broad and jurisdictional limitations would be appropriate for codes with extensive disclosure already imposed on high-level officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This appears to be an internal memorandum rather than a formal advice letter response to an external inquiry. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document summarizes a Committee recommendation rather than providing legal advice to a requestor. Limited document content and handwritten annotations affect confidence.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether gifts from sources outside the jurisdiction of the Coastal Commission need to be disclosed under the Coastal Commission Code.

CONCLUSION: The Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended that gifts from sources outside the Coastal Commission's jurisdiction need not be disclosed, based on the rationale that disclosure requirements under the Code are already broad and jurisdictional limitations would be appropriate for codes with extensive disclosure already imposed on high-level officials.

The justification for this decision was that disclosure under this Code already was very broad. The Committee thought that this type of jurisdictional limitation would be appropriate for Codes in which broad disclosure already has been imposed upon the highest officials and those lower officials who have much contact with persons outside the jurisdiction. embedding.summary: This is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting a Conflicts of Interest Committee decision regarding whether gifts from outside the Coastal Commission's jurisdiction require disclosure under the Coastal Commission Code.
